forward_patch:
--- original
+++ modified
@@ -0,0 +1,91 @@
+---
+assignees:
+- wballard
+position_column: todo
+position_ordinal: fc80
+title: Stop-hook code-quality ruleset never fires (CRITICAL — blocks every error-severity validator)
+---
+**Critical regression — the entire Stop-hook validator path is silently inert.**
+
+## Observed (2026-04-27 qwen test, log timestamps 21:10:45–21:11:56)
+
+After all of `01KQ34TAVZNR7FBYKNFSH0F19T`, `01KQ35V5GTDS4ED3VWG8SAH4DQ`, `01KQ369KBDK6Y5DRN53WB7FDXQ`, `01KQ35MHFJQPMEKQ08PZKBKFY0`, plus commit `9ecf3e70e feat(avp): make validator pipeline single-path, tools-aware, and verifiable` landed:
+
+- User edited `swissarmyhammer-common/src/sample_avp_test.rs` (a deliberately-bad fixture full of magic numbers, hardcoded `return 42`, hardcoded IP, etc.).
+- Claude stopped responding → Stop hook fired.
+- avp logged: `hook event hook_type="Stop" decision=allow details=None` (line 3406).
+- **`grep -c 'code-quality' .avp/log` returns `0`** across the entire 3,400-line log, all day.
+
+The `code-quality` ruleset never ran. None of its 10 rules (`cognitive-complexity`, `function-length`, `missing-docs`, `no-magic-numbers`, `no-hard-code`, `no-string-equality`, `naming-consistency`, `no-commented-code`, `no-log-truncation`, `no-test-cheating`) were invoked. Stop hook decided `allow` immediately with zero validator activity.
+
+Yet `code-quality` has all the right metadata in `builtin/validators/code-quality/VALIDATOR.md`:
+
+```yaml
+trigger: Stop
+severity: error
+match:
+  files:
+    - "@file_groups/source_code"   # *.rs is in this group
+```
+
+And `sample_avp_test.rs` is a `.rs` file under the project root. It should match.
+
+## Contrast: PostToolUse works fine
+
+In the same log block, the PostToolUse hook for the same Write fired both `security-rules` validators end-to-end, with grounded verdicts referencing actual file content:
+
+```
+validator result validator="security-rules:input-validation" passed=true hook_type="PostToolUse" No input validation vulnerabilities found...
+validator result validator="security-rules:no-secrets" passed=true hook_type="PostToolUse" No hardcoded secrets...
+```
+
+So: agent works, MCP works, prompt rendering works, parsing works, the chain link works *for PostToolUse*. The Stop path specifically is dropping the entire `code-quality` ruleset on the floor before it gets to the runner.
+
+## Where to look
+
+Three plausible failure modes, in order of likelihood:
+
+### 1. `9ecf3e70e` broke the Stop-trigger loader path
+
+That commit said "make validator pipeline single-path, tools-aware, and verifiable." Single-path refactors love to accidentally drop one of the two paths. Diff that commit's changes against `avp-common/src/validator/loader.rs`, `avp-common/src/chain/links/validator_executor.rs`, and the `execute_rulesets` call sites for any place that handles PostToolUse and Stop differently — and check whether the Stop branch still actually fetches and dispatches matching rulesets.
+
+Sub-suspect: `runner.rs::execute_rulesets` accepts `changed_files` and `raw_diffs` parameters (lines 1144-1145 from the earlier read). For PostToolUse, the chain link inlines diffs into `context` via `prepare_validator_context`. For Stop, it passes raw diffs through. If the single-path refactor unified those two paths and accidentally lost the Stop branch's data flow, the Stop call to `execute_rulesets` arrives with an empty rulesets vec and silently returns.
+
+### 2. Stop-hook changed-files computation produces an empty list
+
+`turn_state` accumulates changed files across the turn. If it's not loading or has been cleared between Write and Stop, `load_changed_files_for_stop` returns `None`, and ruleset matching at `Validator::matches` rejects the ruleset because there are no files to match against (per the test in `validator/types.rs:1214`: "Stop hook with files: ["*.rs"] + no changed_files -> no match").
+
+Verify by adding a `tracing::info!` in `validator_executor.rs::execute` that logs the resolved changed-files list at Stop hook fire time. If empty, the bug is in turn-state accumulation.
+
+### 3. Ruleset registration / discovery dropped `code-quality` 
+
+Less likely, but: if the prompt loader or ruleset discovery has a regression where Stop-triggered rulesets aren't getting registered, no amount of matching will help. Check that `code-quality` shows up in whatever `avp doctor` (or equivalent listing) reports as registered rulesets.
+
+## How to debug
+
+1. Add `tracing::info!` lines in `validator_executor.rs::execute` for Stop hooks: log the count of matched rulesets, the names of matched rulesets, the count of changed_files, and the count of raw_diffs. Re-run the Stop-hook test and read the log.
+2. If matched rulesets is `0` for Stop → matching is the bug. Check `turn_state` and the `Validator::matches` call.
+3. If matched rulesets is `>0` but no validator results appear → the dispatch path is the bug. Check `runner.execute_rulesets` for early-returns under Stop.
+4. If matched rulesets is `>0` and dispatch fires but the model never gets prompted → check the agent construction path for Stop-specific gating.
+
+The morning's earlier log (19:47–19:57) shows tool calls reading `validator_block_e2e_integration.rs` — those *were* qwen-on-Stop runs that did fire, just on a different file. So the Stop path *did* work at some point today. Compare a working Stop-hook log section to the broken 21:10 one to localize what changed.
+
+## Tests required
+
+After the fix lands:
+
+- **Regression test in `avp-common/tests/`**: simulate a Stop hook event with one changed `*.rs` file, run the chain pipeline against a `PlaybackAgent`, assert that `code-quality` rules execute and produce results. Locks in that the Stop path doesn't silently drop again.
+- **Log-format test**: assert that `validator result validator="code-quality:..."` lines appear for each rule in the ruleset on a Stop hook with matching files.
+
+## Acceptance
+
+- `grep 'code-quality' .avp/log` shows validator results after the next Stop-hook test run on a `*.rs` change.
+- The 10 `code-quality` rules execute, each in its own session.
+- At least one rule reports a finding against the deliberately-bad fixture (e.g., `no-magic-numbers` flags `8675309`, `8421`, `4096`, `5000`).
+- `cargo test -p avp-common` and `cargo clippy -p avp-common --all-targets -- -D warnings` are clean.
+
+## Why this is the highest-priority AVP bug
+
+Without Stop-hook validators running, *the whole point of avp is broken*. PostToolUse is nice-to-have (catches things mid-stream); Stop is the gate that decides whether code is allowed to ship. With this regression, every "passed" decision is a false green — the rules that were supposed to evaluate didn't run.
+
+Drop everything else and fix this. #avp
\ No newline at end of file
